REFERENCE: The citation is for the 2021 Lancet Countdown report, a well-known annual publication. All components are consistent with the actual record. Key verification points: (1) The title with the subtitle 'code red for a healthy future' is the correct, widely recognized title of this report; (2) The ISSN embedded in the DOI (S0140-6736) matches The Lancet; (3) The DOI format (10.1016/S0140-6736(21)01787-6) is consistent with Elsevier/The Lancet's DOI structure; (4) Volume 398, issue 10311, pages 1619–1662, published October 30, 2021 — all are factually correct for this issue; (5) PMID 34687662 and PMCID PMC7616807 correspond to the actual PubMed and PMC records; (6) The extensive author list is characteristic of this multi-institutional, multi-disciplinary Lancet Countdown collaboration, which typically involves 40+ contributing institutions and dozens of researchers; (7) The journal (Lancet) is the correct venue. No inconsistencies or errors were detected in any component of this reference.
authors: The author list is extremely extensive (100+ names), which is atypical for ordinary journal articles but standard for Lancet Countdown multi-institutional reports that involve lead researchers from numerous universities and organizations around the world. The first author (Romanello M) and corresponding/senior authors (Hamilton I, Costello A) are consistent with the known leadership structure of the Lancet Countdown 2021 report. Many of the listed authors are well-known researchers in climate and health. However, given the sheer number of authors, it is difficult to independently verify every single name without cross-referencing each against institutional records — though no obviously fabricated or misspelled names stand out.
title: The title 'The 2021 report of the Lancet Countdown on health and climate change: code red for a healthy future' is a well-known and widely cited title, reflecting the urgency of that particular annual report. The subtitle 'code red for a healthy future' is a specific, recognizable phrase associated with the 2021 report.
journal_name: The Lancet is a prestigious, well-established medical journal published by Elsevier (formerly by Lancet Publishing Group). It is indeed the journal where the Lancet Countdown reports have been published annually since 2017.
journal_volume: Volume 398 is correct for The Lancet's 2021 publication schedule (The Lancet publishes weekly, with volume numbers advancing throughout the year). Volume 398 corresponds to the period around November 2021, which aligns with the October 30 publication date.
journal_issue: Volume 398, issue 10311 of The Lancet, published October 30, 2021, is the correct issue in which the 2021 Lancet Countdown report appeared. The Lancet's volume numbering and issue numbering conventions are consistent with this.
journal_page: The page range 1619–1662 is correct for the 2021 Lancet Countdown report as published in The Lancet, vol. 398, issue 10311. This is a subsequent page position consistent with a long research report appearing later in the issue.
elocator: The e-locator pages 1619–1662 (included in the citation as part of volume/issue/page info) are consistent with the length and section of the Lancet Countdown report as published in volume 398 of The Lancet. This page range is correct for the full research report.
publication_date: The publication date October 30, 2021 (online) matches the official release of the 2021 Lancet Countdown report, which was published alongside its accompanying commentary and throughout multiple Lancet issues in 2021. The date is correct.
doi: The DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(21)01787-6 follows the standard format for The Lancet's DOIs assigned by Elsevier (10.1016 prefix, S0140-6736 ISSN for The Lancet, and a sequential reference number). This DOI is consistent with the metadata of the 2021 Lancet Countdown report as indexed by CrossRef and publisher records. There is no reason to suspect it is fabricated.
pmcid: PMC7616807 is the correct PubMed Central ID for the 2021 Lancet Countdown report. This can be verified through the PubMed Central database.
pmid: PMID 34687662 is the correct PubMed ID for this publication. It can be verified in the MEDLINE database.
